question_text: A light bulb converts 95 J of electrical energy into 5 J of light energy and 90 J of heat energy. What is its efficiency?
explanation: Efficiency = (useful energy output / total energy input) × 100%. Here, useful output is 5 J and total input is 95 J, so efficiency = (5/95) × 100 = 5.3%. This shows why old incandescent bulbs were inefficient at producing light.
